title: Projections are not pushed through unnest(arg_max(...))
description:
### What happens?

Consider this table foo:
```sql
create table foo (
  col_1 text,
  col_2 text,
  col_3 text,
  col_4 text,
  col_5 int
);
```

Consider the common case where you have a log of events and you want to take the latest version of a row you would possibly write something like:
```sql
select col_1, col_2, col_3
from (
  select unnest(arg_max(foo, col_5))
  from foo
  group by col_1, col_2
)
```

`arg_max` will construct a struct of all columns of `foo` and then perform projection after unnesting. But here we can see that adding col_4 to the intermediate struct is unnecessary and can be pruned.

Now imagine a very wide table (100s of columns), performing this `unnest(arg_max())` is very memory intensive where if the downstream projection is limited to only a handful of columns the aggregation performance and memory usage improves.

Currently you must manually specify what you want to project before aggregation which is do-able but tedious:
```sql
select col_1, col_2, col_3
from (
  select unnest(arg_max(t, col_5))
  from (select col_1, col_2, col_3, col_5 from foo) as t
  group by col_1, col_2
)
```

### To Reproduce

```sql
create table foo (
  col_1 text,
  col_2 text,
  col_3 text,
  col_4 text,
  col_5 int
);

explain select col_1, col_2, col_3
from (
  select unnest(arg_max(foo, col_5))
  from foo
  group by col_1, col_2
);

explain select col_1, col_2, col_3
from (
  select unnest(arg_max(t, col_5))
  from (select col_1, col_2, col_3, col_5 from foo) as t
  group by col_1, col_2
);
```

### OS:

arm64

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.2

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Ryan Chui

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE bar (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP, is_active BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO bar (name, value, created_at, is_active) VALUES ('Event A', 10.5, '2023-01-01 10:00:00', TRUE);
INSERT INTO bar (name, value, created_at, is_active) VALUES ('Event B', 20.0, '2023-01-02 11:00:00', FALSE);
SELECT name, value FROM (SELECT * FROM bar WHERE (id, created_at) IN (SELECT id, MAX(created_at) FROM bar GROUP BY is_active)) AS subquery;

SELECT name, value FROM (SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, name, value FROM bar WHERE is_active = TRUE) AS t WHERE (name, value) IN (SELECT name, MAX(value) FROM (SELECT name, value FROM bar WHERE is_active = TRUE) GROUP BY name)) AS subquery;
